meta.description: Complete brand and UI design system for the Humanizer Chrome extension — a local-first writing assistant. Use this file as the single source of truth for all visual decisions in the extension popup, content script UI, and any web-facing surfaces.

logo.description: The Humanizer wordmark icon / app icon
logo.mark_concept: The mark combines a check (✓) for confidence and a subtle pen stroke to represent writing and refinement. Sleek, minimal, and memorable.
logo.shape: Rounded-rectangle app icon with heavy corner radius (~28% of icon width), dark background

symbol.type: Stylized lowercase letter 'e' merged with a checkmark pen stroke
symbol.form: Single continuous rounded stroke that reads as both an 'e' letterform and a check/edit gesture
symbol.orientation: Centered inside icon container, slightly left-weighted

gradient.applies_to: The symbol stroke only — not the background

variants.name: dark_bg
variants.label: Primary — dark background
variants.usage: Default app icon, extension toolbar icon, dark UI contexts

variants.name: light_bg
variants.label: Light background
variants.usage: Light mode surfaces, print, white-background contexts

variants.name: outline
variants.label: Outline / monochrome
variants.usage: Grid/spec views, ghost states, development overlays

construction.note: The mark is NOT a checkmark alone and NOT an 'e' alone — it is the visual union of both concepts

do_not: Do not stretch or distort the mark
Do not place on a busy photographic background without a backing card
Do not recolor the gradient — always use the defined pink-to-violet range
Do not use the symbol without the rounded-rectangle container in app-icon contexts

colors.description: Full color palette extracted from brand sheet. Always use CSS custom properties (var(--color-*)) in code.

primary_gradient.label: Brand gradient — pink to violet
primary_gradient.usage: Primary buttons, active icons, logo mark, key CTAs, progress indicators

palette.name: pink
palette.label: Brand Pink
palette.usage: Gradient start, accent dots, icon active state (Check icon ring), error-adjacent highlights

palette.name: purple
palette.label: Brand Purple
palette.usage: Gradient mid, secondary accents, hover states on primary

palette.name: violet
palette.label: Brand Violet / Indigo
palette.usage: Gradient end, link colors, focus rings, tag backgrounds

palette.name: bg_darkest
palette.label: Background — Darkest
palette.usage: Page/app background, deepest layer, extension popup outer shell

palette.name: bg_dark
palette.label: Background — Dark
palette.usage: Card backgrounds, icon container background, main popup surface

palette.name: bg_mid
palette.label: Background — Mid
palette.usage: Secondary cards, input fields, hover states on dark surfaces, secondary button background

palette.name: text_muted
palette.label: Text — Muted / Light Gray
palette.usage: Body text on dark backgrounds, secondary labels, button text on secondary button

success.note: Brand uses pink check for 'all good' state, not green

grammar_underline.note: Red underline for grammar errors uses brand pink

font_family.note: Inter is the ONLY typeface in the system. Do not introduce additional fonts.

scale.name: heading_2
scale.usage: Card titles, label headings (e.g. COLOR PALETTE, TYPOGRAPHY section labels)

scale.name: label_caps
scale.usage: Section category labels (COLOR PALETTE, TYPOGRAPHY, UI ELEMENTS, ICON STYLE, MARK CONCEPT). Always pink, always uppercase, always tracked.

scale.name: body
scale.usage: Body copy, descriptions, suggestion card text

scale.name: body_small
scale.usage: Helper text, sub-labels, 'No issues found.' status text

scale.name: icon_label
scale.usage: Labels beneath icons in icon grid (Check, Rewrite, Clarity, etc.)

spacing.note: All spacing should be multiples of 4px.

suggestion_card.description: Grammar/clarity suggestion panel shown inline next to underlined text

issue_text.description: Sentence with the problematic phrase underlined

suggestion_row.text: Suggestion text e.g. 'Consider simplifying the structure.'

status_card.description: 'All good / No issues found' clean state card

check_icon.style: circle with checkmark, filled gradient ring (#FF4D8D → #B14DFF)

icons.style: Thin stroke, rounded line caps, minimal fill — NOT filled/solid. Monochrome on dark card backgrounds.

icon_set.name: Check
icon_set.description: Circle with a checkmark inside. Active variant has a pink gradient ring border.
icon_set.usage: Grammar check status, 'All good' indicator, Check feature tab

icon_set.name: Rewrite
icon_set.description: Four-pointed star / sparkle with a small additional sparkle — represents AI magic/rewrite action.
icon_set.usage: Rewrite feature trigger, rewrite tab icon

icon_set.name: Clarity
icon_set.description: Lightbulb outline — represents insight and readability improvement.

icon_set.name: Tone
icon_set.description: Smiley face / emoji face outline — represents tone and personality of writing.

icon_set.name: Structure
icon_set.description: Three horizontal lines with a small settings/filter tick on the right side — represents document structure and organization.

icon_set.name: Settings
icon_set.description: Gear / cog outline — standard settings icon.

grammar_underline.description: Red underline shown beneath grammar/spelling errors in the Gmail compose field
grammar_underline.implementation_note: In Gmail contenteditable, use CSS Highlight API: CSS.highlights.set('humanizer-grammar', new Highlight(...ranges)) with ::highlight(humanizer-grammar) { text-decoration: underline solid #FF4D8D 2px; }

circular_icon_trigger.description: The floating circular icon that appears beside highlighted text when the user selects text (Rewrite / Generate trigger)
circular_icon_trigger.position: Appears at the end of the text selection, slight offset above-right

rewrite_input_panel.description: Small panel that appears after clicking the circular icon — lets user type a rewrite instruction

popup_shell.description: The extension popup window (popup.html) overall container

transitions.button_hover: opacity 120ms ease, box-shadow 120ms ease, transform 120ms ease

css_custom_properties.description: Paste this :root block into your global CSS / content.css / popup CSS

do: Always use Inter as the typeface
Use the pink (#FF4D8D) → purple (#B14DFF) gradient for all primary CTAs
Keep backgrounds layered: darkest (#0E1117) → card (#1A1F2B) → input (#2A2F3F)
Use uppercase tracked pink text (label_caps) for all section category headers
Keep icons thin-stroke and minimal — no filled/bold icon styles
Use the circular gradient trigger for the Rewrite/Generate floating icon
Maintain the pink dot + arrow → pattern for suggestion cards
dont: Don't use green for 'success' — the brand uses pink check marks
Don't introduce any new typefaces beyond Inter
Don't use solid fills on feature icons — keep them outline/stroke style
Don't use light backgrounds — this is a dark-first design system
Don't change the logo gradient colors or orientation
Don't use opacity hacks to create new grays — use the defined bg palette steps
